title: LATAM Medical Imaging Sharing Infrastructure
problem: Medical imaging (X-rays, CT scans, MRIs) in LATAM is siloed. Patients carry CDs/DVDs between facilities. Images lost, degraded, or incompatible. No cloud-based sharing. Radiologists can't easily access prior images for comparison. Referring physicians wait days for reports. Tele-radiology is limited by technical barriers.
solution: Cloud-based medical imaging platform (PACS in the cloud). Secure upload from imaging centers, instant sharing with radiologists and physicians. Zero-footprint viewer accessible from any device. AI-assisted triage and preliminary reads. Integration with hospital radiology systems.
technology: DICOM standards, cloud PACS, zero-footprint viewer, AI image analysis, integration APIs, mobile access
target_customer: Imaging centers, radiology practices, hospital systems, tele-radiology companies, referring physicians
ai_type: Image analysis, anomaly detection, triage prioritization
traction_signals: AI imaging diagnostics proven in US/China
regulatory_notes: Medical device regulations for diagnostic AI, HIPAA-equivalent data protection, DICOM compliance
varden_fit: HIGH FIT - Infrastructure for imaging workflow. Cloud PACS + AI triage is proven model (Aidoc, Viz.ai). Technical but defensible. Recurring revenue from imaging centers.
notes: Medical imaging is technical space but critical infrastructure. Ambra Health proved cloud PACS model in US. LATAM still using CDs. Massive opportunity.
